skill.name: freshdesk-automation
skill.description: Automate Freshdesk support workflows through Rube MCP, including ticket creation, ticket updates, searches, replies, notes, contacts, and companies.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to manage Freshdesk tickets, conversations, contacts, and company records.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby/shell and weak-crypto hits, but review found a Markdown-only skill with inline MCP tool names, not executable code. The confirmed risk is operational: the skill directs agents to use Rube MCP and Freshdesk to read, create, update, reply to, and import customer support records. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious exfiltration instructions, or executable payloads.

medium_findings.title: Freshdesk Data Modification Through External MCP
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to connect Rube MCP and use Freshdesk tools to create tickets, update tickets, send public replies, add notes, manage contacts, manage companies, and import contacts. These actions can change production support records and customer-visible communications, so users must confirm authorization before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly name Freshdesk tools that create, update, reply, add notes, import contacts, and manage company data. The risk is confirmed operational impact, not malicious code execution.

medium_findings.title: Customer Support Data Exposure Through Searches
medium_findings.description: The skill guides agents to search tickets, view ticket details, list conversations, search contacts, and list company records. Those workflows may expose customer messages, contact details, requester data, and internal notes through an authenticated Freshdesk connection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited workflows directly retrieve ticket, conversation, contact, and company data. Exposure depends on the connected Freshdesk account permissions, but the data-access capability is clear.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External MCP Endpoint
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This appears to be a legitimate setup instruction for the intended integration, but it means execution depends on a third-party network service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is visible and matches the stated Rube MCP setup flow. It is a real network dependency, but no credential exfiltration or unauthorized endpoint is present.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby/shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code references to MCP tool names, parameters, and examples. No evidence found of shell execution, command construction, or executable script content in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the flagged backticks wrap tool names and parameter labels. There is no Ruby code, shell code, subprocess call, or command execution syntax to execute.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm findings do not correspond to cryptographic code or algorithm use. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic library calls in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited file contains descriptive Markdown and Freshdesk workflow guidance only. The scanner likely matched short text fragments, not real weak cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Support teams often lose time switching between ticket, contact, and company tasks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured Freshdesk workflow through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for a Rube MCP Freshdesk connection.
Creates, updates, views, searches, and filters Freshdesk tickets.
Adds public replies and private or public notes to tickets.
Searches contacts and companies, then resolves display values to IDs.
Documents Freshdesk parameter rules, pagination limits, and common endpoint quirks.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and authenticated Freshdesk account.
Does not include executable code, tests, or custom validation logic.
Depends on current Freshdesk and Rube tool schemas at runtime.
Cannot bypass Freshdesk permissions, rate limits, or account plan limits.

use_cases.title: Create and Triage Tickets
use_cases.description: Create new tickets, assign priority, set status, and verify ticket details before handoff.

use_cases.title: Manage Customer Conversations
use_cases.description: Review ticket history, send customer replies, and add internal notes with correct visibility.

use_cases.title: Maintain Account Records
use_cases.description: Search contacts and companies, resolve IDs, and update company metadata for support workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Ticket
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Freshdesk automation skill to create a ticket for this customer. Confirm the requester email, subject, description, priority, and source before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Find Urgent Open Tickets
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Freshdesk for open high-priority or urgent tickets from the last seven days. Summarize ticket IDs, requester context, priority, and next action.

prompt_templates.title: Reply With Internal Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this ticket and conversation history, draft a customer reply, and add a private note that explains the internal reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Reconcile Support Account Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the requester contact and company record for this ticket. Resolve IDs, identify missing company fields, and propose safe updates before making changes.

output_examples.input: Create a ticket for a customer reporting failed password resets.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms requester identity, creates the ticket with the correct priority, and returns the ticket ID with a short next-step summary.

output_examples.input: Find pending tickets for a specific requester email.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches the contact, resolves the requester ID, filters matching tickets, and summarizes status, priority, assignee, and age.

output_examples.input: Add an internal note after reviewing a billing issue.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies the ticket, drafts an agent-only note, confirms visibility, and reports that the note was added.

best_practices: Call the Rube tool search first so current Freshdesk schemas are used.
Confirm requester identity and ticket IDs before changing records or sending replies.
Use private notes for internal reasoning unless a public customer message is intended.
anti_patterns: Do not send public replies before reviewing the ticket state and recipients.
Do not run broad list-all workflows when a filtered search can answer the request.
Do not import contacts or update companies without explicit authorization.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown workflow guide for using Freshdesk through Rube MCP tools.

faq.question: What connection is required?
faq.answer: Users need Rube MCP configured and an active Freshdesk connection through the Freshdesk toolkit.

faq.question: Can it send customer-visible messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents Freshdesk reply tools, so users should confirm content and recipients before sending.

faq.question: Can it manage contacts and companies?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers contact search, contact import, company search, company creation, and company updates.

faq.question: Does it handle Freshdesk custom fields?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains ticket custom field prefixes and notes that company custom fields use a different format.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The file is not malicious, but authenticated workflows can read and modify live customer support data.
